sections.label: The Guiding Principle
sections.html: “AI can help you understand and improve your work, but the work itself must be yours. Think of AI like a tutor, not a ghostwriter.”
AI tools like ChatGPT, Claude, Copilot, and others are part of the modern cybersecurity landscape. This policy defines how you can use them ethically and effectively in this course.

items.title: Explaining command syntax
items.desc: Ask AI to explain what a command does or how flags work

items.title: Troubleshooting errors
items.desc: Paste an error message and ask for debugging help

items.title: Understanding concepts
items.desc: Ask AI to explain ideas from the textbook differently

items.title: Proofreading & grammar
items.desc: Have AI check your report writing for clarity

items.title: Suggesting structure
items.desc: Ask for feedback on report organization

items.title: Writing report sections
items.desc: AI cannot write your executive summaries or findings

items.title: Answering quiz questions
items.desc: Quizzes verify you did the hands-on work

items.title: Generating fake screenshots
items.desc: All evidence must come from your actual VM

items.title: Completing labs for you
items.desc: You must run the tools yourself

items.title: Fabricating tool output
items.desc: Real results only, even if they’re incomplete

sections.label: Why This Matters in Cybersecurity
paragraphs: In professional security work, your findings must be defensible and reproducible. If you tell a client they have a critical vulnerability, you need to prove it. AI-generated fabrications could lead to false positives (wasting client resources) or false negatives (missing real threats).
The skills you build here—running tools, interpreting output, documenting methodology—are exactly what employers and certifications test. Shortcuts now create gaps that will hurt you later.

ok.label: ✓ OK to Ask
items: "What does the -sV flag do in Nmap?"
"Why am I getting ‘permission denied’ when running this?"
"How should I organize my findings section?"

notOk.label: ✗ Not OK to Ask
items: "Write an executive summary for my Nmap scan"
"What would Wireshark show if I captured DNS traffic?"

sections.label: The Bottom Line:
sections.html: Use AI to understand concepts and tools. Don’t use it to produce your deliverables. The goal is to make YOU a security professional, not to make you good at prompting AI.
Questions about what’s allowed? Ask in Discord or email.
